summary: Broad item decompilation pass aimed at getting item translation units over 60% fuzzy match, with the PR body noting "some new linkages". The diff implements many previously stubbed item functions, adds item state tables and concrete item-var/attribute structs, fixes symbol scope/local labels, and flips itfreeze.c, itpikachutjoltground.c, ithinoarashi.c, and ittincle.c from NonMatching to Matching in configure.py. The automated decomp.dev report credited the PR with 48 new matches, +10876 matched-code bytes, +15096 linked-code bytes, +1008 matched-data bytes, and +712 linked-data bytes.
what_changed: configure.py now marks src/melee/it/items/itfreeze.c, itpikachutjoltground.c, ithinoarashi.c, and ittincle.c as Matching instead of NonMatching.
Implemented many previously placeholder/stubbed item functions across Freeze, Flipper, Hinoarashi, Kirby 2F23, Kyasarin, Ness yoyo, Pikachu Thunder, Pikachu TJolt ground, Tincle, and Yoshi Egg Throw.

Added or materialized multiple ItemStateTable definitions, including it_803F5D28 for Freeze, it_803F8270/it_803F82A0 for Hinoarashi, it_803F90D0 for Kyasarin, it_803F70C8 for Pikachu Thunder, it_803F7190 for Pikachu TJolt ground, it_803F9000 for Tincle, and it_803F7118 for Yoshi Egg Throw.

Expanded item variable and attribute structs: examples include itNessYoyo_ItemVars, itPikachutJoltGround_ItemVars/Attributes, itHinoarashi_ItemVars/Attributes, itTincle_ItemVars/Attributes, itKyasarinAttributes, itYoshiEggThrowAttributes, and Oldottosea x20 becoming Item_GObj*.
Typed yoyo resource fields in itYoyoAttributes from generic s32 values to HSD_Joint* and HSD_MatAnimJoint* members used by Ness yoyo string/yoyo setup.
Added the nessyoyo member to the Item xDD4_itemVar union in src/melee/it/types.h, replacing previous alias-like accesses through unrelated item vars in itnessyoyo.c.
Updated headers from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS or loose types to concrete signatures such as Item_GObj*, HSD_GObj*, Vec3*, s32, f32, and bool where the diff provided evidence.
Adjusted config/GALE01/symbols.txt to localize several functions/data constants/strings, converting global it_804D.../it_803F... symbols to local @NNN labels and marking some functions scope:local.
smart_moves: The PR treated matching as code, data, headers, and symbol-scope work together rather than only filling in function bodies.
Only the four item objects that became linkable were flipped to Matching in configure.py; other improved but still fuzzy TUs remained unpromoted.

Concrete item-var and attribute typing reduced unsafe casts and union aliasing, especially in Ness yoyo where samusgrapple/flipper/foxillusion-style field reuse was replaced by a dedicated nessyoyo layout.
ItemStateTable definitions were added near their callback implementations, which helped both data matching and object linkage.

Static inline helpers were used to share repeated logic while preserving compiler code shape, for example in Flipper damage/bounce code, Freeze reset/clear helpers, Kyasarin animation transitions, Pikachu Thunder scaling, and Kirby 2F23 update/scale logic.
Symbol-localization in symbols.txt matched the source-level static/local intent and prevented local strings/constants/floats from being treated as global data.
decomp_lessons: For item TUs, raising fuzzy match often requires validating the xDD4 item-var layout and xC4 special-attributes layout before the code will naturally match.
Dedicated item union members are safer and more match-friendly than borrowing unrelated item-var layouts for same-offset storage.
Header signatures matter: replacing UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS with exact Item_GObj/HSD_GObj/Vec3/s32/f32/bool types can unlock cleaner source and cross-file linkage.
Data matching can depend on symbol scope and naming as much as on bytes; local constants and strings should be represented as local @NNN symbols when evidence supports it.
When promoting objects to Matching, update configure.py only after the object is actually linkable and data ordering/symbols are accounted for.
Automated progress reports are useful evidence: this PR had no human review comments, but the decomp.dev bot quantified exact matched/linked code and data gains.
naming_conventions: Unknown item functions remained address-based with the it_802... pattern when no semantic name was established.
Motion callbacks followed the existing it<Item>_UnkMotionN_Anim/Phys/Coll convention, such as itFreeze_UnkMotion0_Coll and itPikachutjoltground_UnkMotion1_Phys.
Item logic callbacks kept item-logic naming like it_2725_Logic106_Reflected, it_2725_Logic106_ShieldBounced, it_2725_Logic43_Clanked, and it_3F14_Logic20_DmgReceived.

Struct fields used offset-style xNN names with comments preserving item-var or attribute offsets; more specific names were used when evidence was clear, such as x50_string_joint, x54_yoyo_joint, and x58_yoyo_matanim.
Header prototypes kept address comments like /* 2B338C */ and were updated to concrete types instead of generic unknown macros.
Local data in config/GALE01/symbols.txt used @NNN labels, while global item symbols retained it_... or ItemStateTable_... names.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used PAD_STACK and explicit u8 _pad locals in several functions to preserve stack layout.
Split functions into static inline helpers to force inlining and maintain repeated code shape without duplicating large blocks by hand.
Used exact math/library forms such as sqrtf__Ff, my_sqrtf with __frsqrte, fabs, atan2f, sinf, cosf, M_PI, M_PI_2, and M_TAU where needed for compiler output.
Added static data_ordering functions in item files such as itpikachutjoltground.c and ittincle.c to influence literal/data ordering.
Marked local callbacks/static functions consistently, including itPikachutjoltground_UnkMotion1_Phys and itHinoarashi_UnkMotion2_Anim becoming local/static in the symbol/source evidence.
Used a fake_HSD_JObjSetRotationX inline in Kyasarin to match direct JObj rotation field writes plus HSD_ASSERT behavior rather than relying on a non-matching library call shape.
Materialized ItemStateTable arrays in source so .data sections could match rather than relying on anonymous/global symbols.
Replaced casts through s32 or unrelated item-var structs with typed pointers such as Item_GObj*, ItemLink*, HSD_GObj*, HSD_JObj*, HSD_Joint*, and HSD_MatAnimJoint*.
review_feedback: No human review comments or reviews were present in the slice.

The only PR comment was the automated decomp.dev report, which reported GALE01 matched code 63.74% (+0.28%, +10876 bytes), linked code 35.40% (+0.39%, +15096 bytes), matched data 38.25% (+0.08%, +1008 bytes), linked data 32.58% (+0.06%, +712 bytes), 48 new matches, and 18 improvements in unmatched items.
